summary: mcp-remote command injection enables credential theft across 437K environments
details: mcp-remote versions prior to 1.7.0 contain an OS command injection vulnerability in the OAuth authorization flow. When connecting to an untrusted MCP server, crafted input from the authorization_endpoint response URL enables arbitrary command execution. Attackers can steal API keys, cloud credentials, SSH keys, and Git repository contents from any environment running the vulnerable package.

agent_threat.attack_vector: Crafted authorization_endpoint URL triggers OS command injection during OAuth flow
mitigations: Scan MCP servers with cybersecify before connecting
Use mcp-secure verifyTool() to detect modified server definitions
